reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag a target into Lunox's burst range.
Lunox benefits from a fixed target because her damage window is strongest when the enemy cannot immediately disengage.

proof.summary: Kaja locks a target with suppression, while Lunox follows with high magic burst during the control window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches a priority target instead of a frontliner.
Lunox has Chaos burst ready before Kaja commits.
failureCases: Kaja pulls the target away from Lunox.
Lunox has already used her key damage or survival skill.
Enemy peel interrupts Lunox after Kaja engages.

reasons: Franco can hook or suppress one target, giving Lunox a safe opening to burst.
Lunox can punish the target Franco displaces before the enemy team fully responds.

proof.summary: Franco isolates a target with displacement or suppression, while Lunox delivers burst into the locked target.
worksBestWhen: Franco hooks a damage dealer or fragile target.
Lunox waits nearby with damage cooldowns ready.
failureCases: Lunox is not in range after the hook.

reasons: Chou can displace a priority target into Lunox's burst range.
Lunox can punish the target immediately after Chou's kick creates a fixed position.

proof.summary: Chou isolates and kicks a target into a punishable position, while Lunox uses Darkening burst to finish the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Chou kicks a squishy or high-value target toward Lunox.
Lunox waits for Chou's confirmed displacement before spending burst.
failureCases: Chou kicks the target away from Lunox.
Lunox is forced to use Brilliance before the target arrives.

reasons: Kalea can help isolate or hold a target long enough for Lunox to enter her burst window.
Kalea also provides sustain utility, which helps Lunox survive after committing forward.

proof.summary: Kalea creates a local control window, while Lunox follows with Darkening burst on the controlled target.
worksBestWhen: Kalea catches a priority target close enough for Lunox to follow.
Lunox has Darkening available and does not need to use Brilliance first.
failureCases: Kalea controls a target outside Lunox's range.
Lunox is zoned before she can enter.
The enemy team counter-engages after Lunox commits.

reasons: Carmilla's linked ultimate can make Lunox's damage pressure matter across multiple enemies.
Lunox benefits when enemies are linked or grouped because she can pressure one target while still contributing to the larger fight.

proof.summary: Carmilla links enemies, while Lunox applies magic damage and slow pressure into the linked fight.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla lands Curse of Blood on multiple heroes.
Lunox uses burst after the link is active.
failureCases: Lunox is forced to use Brilliance defensively before dealing damage.
Enemies split and avoid shared pressure.
